metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, reconnect_line, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[0]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=5
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is non-empty. Include one reconnect_line candidate only for a currently disconnected line when cooldown allows, so the simulator can test restoration safety.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"reconnect_line","line_id":0,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"test reconnecting a currently disconnected line"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=4
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=2
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=1
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

step=5/20

line_count=20
generator_count=6

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, reconnect_line, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[0]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=5
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is non-empty. Include one reconnect_line candidate only for a currently disconnected line when cooldown allows, so the simulator can test restoration safety.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"reconnect_line","line_id":0,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"test reconnecting a currently disconnected line"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=3
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=2
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, reconnect_line, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[0]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=4
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is non-empty. Include one reconnect_line candidate only for a currently disconnected line when cooldown allows, so the simulator can test restoration safety.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":9,"rho":0.839}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"reconnect_line","line_id":0,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"test reconnecting a currently disconnected line"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=3
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":9,"rho":0.8309}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=emergency; emergency_window_steps_remaining=1
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":9,"rho":0.8295}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.

metadata.teacher_prompt:
You are a grid operator proposing actions for a deterministic simulator.
This is the proposal stage of a two-stage control loop. Do not choose one final action here. Propose exactly 3 candidate actions for the physics sandbox to test.
The simulator will execute rollouts for all 3 candidates and the next stage will choose the safest executable option. If you return only one action, the simulator has no alternatives and the response is invalid.
For tasks using a candidates array, the top-level JSON key must be "candidates" and the value must be an array containing exactly 3 action objects.
Each candidate must be a full action object with action_type, line_id, gen_id, delta_mw, and reason. Do not place action_type directly at the top level unless the schema uses primary_action/backup_action fields.
Allowed candidate action types for this proposal: redispatch, do_nothing.
TASK RULE: In n_minus_1, operate the degraded topology safely for 20 steps. Reconnect the faulted line when cooldown allows and when simulation shows it is safe.
FAULTED_LINE=0; disconnected_now=[]
N-1 PHASE=steady_state; emergency_window_steps_remaining=0
EMERGENCY OBJECTIVE: In steps 1-5, prioritize actions that bring max_rho below 0.92 as fast as possible. Clearing the emergency window is the top priority.
STEADY-STATE OBJECTIVE: From step 6 onward, prioritize keeping max_rho below 0.90 on as many steps as possible while preserving survivability.
RECONNECTION OBJECTIVE: disconnected_now is empty. Do not propose reconnect_line; reconnecting an already connected line wastes a simulator slot and will be rejected.
CANDIDATE RULE: In the emergency phase, include at least two distinct redispatch candidates aimed at immediate rho reduction when no reconnect candidate is legal. Do not fill the set with passive do_nothing-style choices.
CANDIDATE RULE: If no action looks clearly better, still propose the smallest legal redispatch test instead of an illegal reconnect or repeated do_nothing candidate.
ILLEGAL ACTION RULE: New disconnect_line actions are blocked in n_minus_1 even if blocked_sensitivity_guidance says a disconnect would reduce rho. Do not convert blocked disconnect suggestions into reconnect_line.
N-1 STRUCTURAL SECURITY: score=0.000; bridge_lines=[]
THRESHOLDS: EMERGENCY if any line rho >= 0.92, WARNING for 0.80 <= rho < 0.92, SAFE if all lines are below 0.80.
EMERGENCY_LINES=[]
WARNING_LINES=[{"line_id":9,"rho":0.8177}]
RECONNECT_WINDOW_LINES=[]
Allowed for this task: redispatch, reconnect_line for currently disconnected lines, and do_nothing. New disconnect_line actions are blocked.
Return valid RFC 8259 JSON only.
Use this exact JSON shape: {"candidates":[{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":0,"delta_mw":5.0,"reason":"test an allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"redispatch","line_id":null,"gen_id":1,"delta_mw":-5.0,"reason":"test a different allowed redispatch candidate"},{"action_type":"do_nothing","line_id":null,"gen_id":null,"delta_mw":null,"reason":"baseline candidate"}]}
Rules: all property names must be double-quoted; use JSON null, not None; no markdown, no prose, no code fences, no extra keys, exactly 3 candidates.
Field rule: redispatch requires integer gen_id and numeric delta_mw; do_nothing requires line_id=null, gen_id=null, delta_mw=null; line switching requires integer line_id and null gen_id/delta_mw.
Candidate diversity is important because bad candidates are rejected by simulation. Include meaningfully different legal options that are legal in the current state.
Diversity rule: use at least two different action types when plausible.
CRITICAL PHYSICS RULE: Prioritize usable_sensitivity_guidance, not blocked_sensitivity_guidance. A mathematically helpful action is still invalid if the current task forbids that action type.
